title: Defect Spectrum of the Baker Map Under Vertical-Strip Observable Projection
statement: For the specified finite Ulam discretisations of the Baker map with a vertical-strip observable projection, the computed defect operator exhibits a stable singular spectrum with two unit singular values followed by numerically zero singular values.

definitions.name: Baker map
definitions.description: Piecewise-linear expanding map used as the dynamical system under investigation.

definitions.name: Ulam discretisation
definitions.description: Finite matrix approximation of the transfer operator using a uniform grid partition.

definitions.name: Observable defect operator
definitions.description: Operator measuring the component of the one-step evolution that leaves the chosen observable subspace.

evidence.description: Executable computation of Ulam matrix, observable projection, and defect singular values.

markov_property.criterion: Ulam matrix columns sum to one within numerical tolerance

resolution_stability.observation: The computed leading singular values remained stable to floating-point precision across tested resolutions.

supported: The selected observable projection is not invariant under the computed evolution operator.
The defect has a low-rank numerical signature for the tested discretisations.
The leading defect singular values are stable across the tested resolutions.
not_claimed: Universal convergence of all Ulam defect spectra.
A general theorem for all Baker map partitions.
That the defect spectrum directly measures mixing rate.
That the vertical-strip observable is a Koopman-invariant subspace.

history.description: Initial AQARION claim entry for Baker map defect spectrum observation.
